mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength and robust corporate revenue growth, leading to upward revisions in earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth post-2010
failure_condition: A 3-month moving average of PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is above a neutral threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are not statistically significantly lower (p > 0.05) in periods when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5% compared to when it is below.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a strong, confident consumer. This joint condition of high spending power and willingness to spend drives corporate revenues and positive market sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: A model using the interaction of RSAFS momentum and UNRATE level fails to outperform a model using only the individual series in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, an unemployment rate (UNRATE) falling below its natural rate signals an overheating labor market. This leads to wage inflation, corporate margin compression, and expectations of Fed tightening, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE is below 4.5% are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected drop in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors, serving as a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown and negatively impacting short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a component of the Coincident Economic Activity Index
failure_condition: Instances of INDPRO monthly drops greater than 0.5% are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This creates policy uncertainty and expectations of future aggressive tightening, causing risk-off sentiment and negative returns.
failure_condition: The specified trigger condition does not lead to a statistically significant negative information coefficient for 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects both strong consumer confidence and favorable credit conditions. This strength in a key cyclical sector has positive spillover effects on the broader economy, supporting equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a major interest-rate sensitive sector
failure_condition: The 6-month moving average of HOUST does not have a persistent positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the most comprehensive signal of economic expansion. This directly translates into higher aggregate corporate earnings, justifying higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarters with higher-than-average real GDP growth are not followed by quarters with positive S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances.
